evals.name: new-feature-should-trigger-skill-check
evals.prompt: I want to add a new feature to my app - a user profile page with avatar upload
evals.expected_output: Should invoke a skill (like brainstorming or planning) before diving into implementation. Should NOT immediately start coding or ask clarifying questions without checking for relevant skills first.

assertions.name: checks_for_skills_before_responding
assertions.description: Claude should invoke or check for relevant skills before providing any response

assertions.name: does_not_rationalize_skipping
assertions.description: Claude should not use phrases like 'just a simple question' or 'let me first explore' as excuses to skip skill checking

evals.name: simple-question-still-checks-skills
evals.prompt: Quick question - what's the difference between let and const in JavaScript?
evals.expected_output: Even for simple questions, Claude should briefly acknowledge checking for relevant skills (or note that no skill applies after checking) before answering.

assertions.name: does_not_skip_due_to_simplicity
assertions.description: Should not assume 'simple question' means no skill check needed

assertions.name: provides_answer_after_check
assertions.description: After checking, should provide a clear answer about let vs const

evals.name: bug-fix-follows-priority
evals.prompt: There's a bug in my authentication middleware - users are getting logged out randomly
evals.expected_output: Should check for debugging/process skills first (before implementation skills), following the priority rule: process skills before implementation skills.

assertions.name: prioritizes_process_skills
assertions.description: Should invoke debugging or process skill before any implementation-specific skill

assertions.name: does_not_jump_to_code
assertions.description: Should not immediately start writing code without following a debugging process
